SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core

Class ScatterGatherFailoverHandler<T,R>

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.ScatterGatherFailoverHandler<T,R>
Type Parameters:
T - composite payload type (the type seen by the annotated method)
R - slice payload type (the type stored/recovered per slice)
All Implemented Interfaces:
FailoverHandler<T>
public class ScatterGatherFailoverHandler<T,R> extends Object implements FailoverHandler<T>
FailoverHandler decorator that adds scatter/gather routing to Failover-annotated methods.

When Failover.payloadSplitter() is non-empty:

  • store — splits the composite payload into individual slices and stores each slice via delegateR.
  • recover — splits the composite key into per-slice keys, recovers each slice via delegateR, then merges the slices back into the composite type via the PayloadSplitter.

When Failover.payloadSplitter() is empty, both operations delegate unchanged to delegateT.

Collaborators

This class is a thin facade: it owns the pass-through decision and delegates the actual work to focused, package-private collaborators (audit A-2):

  • PayloadScatter — the store/scatter side;
  • PayloadGather — the recover/gather side;
  • SliceDispatcher — parallel-vs-sequential slice dispatch and per-slice timeout;
  • SplitterInvoker — splitter lookup and user-exception-wrapping invocation.

Parallel scatter

Passing a non-null Executor enables parallel slice dispatch using CompletableFuture. Slice futures are collected with CompletableFuture.allOf, so any slice failure propagates as an exception on the calling thread.

Context propagator

When parallel execution is enabled, the ContextPropagator wraps each slice task so that thread-bound context (tenant ID, security principal, MDC) is captured on the calling thread and restored on the executor thread before the slice runs. The no-op propagator (ContextPropagator.noOp()) is used by default when no propagator is needed.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Method Details

    • builder

      public static <T, R> ScatterGatherFailoverHandler.Builder<T,R> builder(FailoverHandler<T> delegateT, FailoverHandler<R> delegateR, PayloadSplitterLookup<T,R> payloadSplitterLookup)
      Creates a builder seeded with the three required collaborators.
      Type Parameters:
      T - composite payload type
      R - slice payload type
      Parameters:
      delegateT - handler for composite-type operations
      delegateR - handler for slice-type operations
      payloadSplitterLookup - lookup for the named PayloadSplitter
      Returns:
      a new ScatterGatherFailoverHandler.Builder

    • store

      public T store(@NonNull Failover failover, @NonNull Method method, List<Object> args, T payload)
      Stores payload via scatter when Failover.payloadSplitter() is configured; otherwise delegates unchanged to delegateT. Returns null immediately if payload is null.
      Specified by:
      store in interface FailoverHandler<T>
      Parameters:
      failover - the Failover annotation on the intercepted method
      method - the reflected intercepted method (never null)
      args - the method arguments
      payload - the method return value to store; null is a no-op
      Returns:
      the original payload (scatter path) or the delegate's return value (pass-through path)
      Throws:
      PayloadSplitterNotFoundException - if Failover.payloadSplitter() names a bean that does not exist

    • recover

      public @Nullable T recover(@NonNull Failover failover, @NonNull Method method, List<Object> args, Class<T> clazz, Throwable cause)
      Recovers the composite payload via gather when Failover.payloadSplitter() is configured; otherwise delegates unchanged to delegateT.

      In scatter mode each slice is recovered individually via delegateR and the results are merged back into a T by the PayloadSplitter.merge(java.util.List<com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.splitter.RecoverContext<R>>) method. Returns null if the splitter's merge produces a context with a null payload.

      Specified by:
      recover in interface FailoverHandler<T>
      Parameters:
      failover - the Failover annotation on the intercepted method
      method - the reflected intercepted method (never null)
      args - the method arguments (used to derive per-slice keys via the splitter)
      clazz - the composite return type T
      cause - the exception thrown by the primary method invocation
      Returns:
      the recovered composite payload, or null if recovery produced no data
      Throws:
      PayloadSplitterNotFoundException - if Failover.payloadSplitter() names a bean that does not exist

    • clean

      public void clean()
      Triggers expiry cleanup on both delegateT and delegateR.

      When both delegates are the same instance (the common auto-configured case), clean() is called only once; distinct delegates are each cleaned.

      Specified by:
      clean in interface FailoverHandler<T>
